channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Trump to downsize U.S. military presence in Germany
item.dc:creator: Noah Robertson, Michael Birnbaum, Adam Taylor
item.description: The decision coincides with a feud between the president and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, who has criticized the U.S. war in Iran.

item.title: Direct flights to Venezuela resume, but many still can’t go home
item.dc:creator: Samantha Schmidt
item.description: Passengers on the first direct flight from Miami to Caracas since 2019 were excited to return but anxious about what they would find. For others, passport issues are still a hurdle.

item.title: U.K. investigates attacks on Jewish targets for possible links to Iran
item.dc:creator: Greg Miller
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the attacks after two men were stabbed in a predominantly Jewish neighborhood of north London.

item.title: Russia scales back Victory Day plans as Ukraine’s military reach expands
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: Moscow is reducing the footprint of its foremost annual military parade amid a wave of Ukrainian drone attacks inside Russia.

item.title: Mali’s junta asked Russians to bring order. Militants just stormed in.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason
item.description: Al-Qaeda-linked fighters killed the defense minister, a top Moscow ally, and forced Russian mercenaries to retreat, highlighting the Mali-Russia partnership’s failure.
